name: Party Sushi Rolls
recipeIngredient: 3 cups Water (or according to rice package directions)
3 1/2 tablespoons Rice Vinegar - or - White Vinegar
recipeInstructions:
Sushi, a representative food of Japan, is vinegared rice garnished with an assortment of fresh vegetables, fish and seasonings. Many ingredients...and the bamboo mat...can be found at an Oriental food store.
RICE:
Cook rice in water for about 25 minutes or until all liquid is absorbed. Spread rice evenly over bottom of large glass or wooden bowl.
Mix vinegar, sugar and salt together in small bowl.
Sprinkle vinegar mixture generously over rice. You may not need all the vinegar mixture; rice should remain somewhat dry.
With a large wooden spoon, mix rice with slicing motion. Leave rice in bowl, covered with damp cloth.
ROLLS:
1 pack Nori Seaweed, large or medium (Quality nori seaweed must be crisp; look for well-sealed package)
1 Bamboo Mat, medium or large, for rolling sushi
FILLINGS: Choose from combinations such as:
1 cucumber, cut into thin strips the same length as seaweed
1 avocado, peeled, pitted and cut into thin strips
Fresh cilantro, minced
2 oz daikon radish, cut into strips
2 oz pickled ginger
4 oz small shrimp, cooked
4 oz prosciutto
Cucumber, cut into thin strips
Avocado, peeled, pitted and cut into thin strips
Pickled ginger
To assemble sushi rolls:
Place one sheet of nori seaweed on bamboo mat.
Spread about 3/4 cup rice onto seaweed; flatten with back of spoon. Leave uncovered 3/8 inch (1 cm) at front and end of sheet.
Place all filling ingredients in center.
To form roll, lift one end of the mat and begin to roll, compressing seaweed. Use mat only to help form the roll; it may be difficult initially to keep mat from rolling with seaweed.
Moisten end of seaweed with small amount of water to seal roll.
Cut into rounds approximately 1/2 inch thick.
Yield: 10 servings; 5 rolls, 6 pieces per roll.
